item.title: GCC wants a constitution developed by the people, for the people
Concerns raised over incorrect road markings and illegal parking in Nausori Town
item.description: Concerns have been raised over alleged incorrect road markings, lack of road signs and illegal parking in parts of Nausori Town.A

item.title: $1.78 million evacuation centre marks major milestone for Tamavua Village
item.description: Villagers of Tamavua will soon have a state-of-the-art evacuation centre following the groundbreaking ceremony for the $1.78 milli

item.title: Naivalurua commends Beqa graduates for commitment to building a safer and stronger Fiji
item.description: More than 10 participants successfully completed training under the Solesolevaki ni Veitaqomaki initiative at Raviravi Village on Beqa Island and the Minister for Policing, Ioane Naivalurua has acknowledged their commitment. Naivalurua , who officiated as the chief guest at the graduation ceremony, thanked the graduates for their dedication and contribution towards building a better nation for fut
item.content:encoded:
More than 10 participants successfully completed training under the Solesolevaki ni Veitaqomaki initiative at Raviravi Village on Beqa Island and the Minister for Policing, Ioane Naivalurua has acknowledged their commitment.

Naivalurua , who officiated as the chief guest at the graduation ceremony, thanked the graduates for their dedication and contribution towards building a better nation for future generations.

He says the government recognises the commitment shown by the participants and their willingness to play a role in creating safer and stronger communities.

The Minister says the purpose of the initiative is to build, progress, sustain and achieve prosperity for the future.

Naivalurua stressed that this responsibility must be shared individually, collectively as a vanua, and as a nation to ensure a secure and prosperous Fiji.

The Solesolevaki ni Veitaqomaki initiative aims to strengthen community participation in promoting safety, security and national development.

The graduates successfully completed nationally recognised maritime training aimed at enhancing maritime safety, strengthening technical skills, and building the capacity of island communities to safely operate and maintain vessels while improving their resilience and self-reliance.

item.title: FEO rejects Saneem's allegations as being without legal or factual basis
item.description: The Fijian Elections Office confirms that its legal representative, Shailend Krishna of Krishna & Co Lawyers have formally respond

item.title: Wildfire in southern Spain leaves at least 12 dead and 23 missing
Saneem says FEO’s rejection of $2M demand based on incorrect interpretation of law
item.description: Former Supervisor of Elections Mohammed Saneem has stressed that the Chief Justice’s ruling is crystal clear after the Fijian Elections Office rejected his $2 million demand issued on 29th June this year over alleged malicious prosecution in public office. Saneem and former Minister for Economy Aiyaz Sayed-Khaiyum were acquitted by Chief Justice Salesi Temo on charges relating to receiving corrupt
item.content:encoded:
Former Supervisor of Elections Mohammed Saneem has stressed that the Chief Justice’s ruling is crystal clear after the Fijian Elections Office rejected his $2 million demand issued on 29th June this year over alleged malicious prosecution in public office.

Saneem and former Minister for Economy Aiyaz Sayed-Khaiyum were acquitted by Chief Justice Salesi Temo on charges relating to receiving corrupt benefit and abuse of office.

Saneem says the FEO's response is, once again, founded on an incorrect interpretation of the law and a misunderstanding of their own standing in this matter.

He says Section 18 of the Electoral Act 2014 required any complaint of this kind to be reported in writing to FICAC but in this case, it was not.

Saneem says instead, it was taken to the Police, a body given no role whatsoever under that section.

He stresses the immunity and independence arguments now raised cannot survive that simple fact.

Saneem further stresses that he will not be pursuing this as a single, joint claim against a faceless office, but will be pursuing his claims against each of them individually, on the basis of their own individual conduct and their own individual decisions.
